relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the architecture of presence.
Only the willing heart gives the contribution.
Gold, silver, and bronze become materials of dwelling.
Blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen enter the sacred pattern.
Skins and acacia wood prepare portable holiness.
Oil, spices, and incense prepare light and fragrance.
Stones prepare remembrance and priestly bearing.
Make me a sanctuary, and I will dwell in their midst.
The mountain pattern becomes wilderness craft.
The ark is shaped from acacia wood.
Gold covers inside and outside.
Rings prepare the ark for movement.
Gold-covered poles are made.
The ark will be carried by the poles.
The poles stay with the ark: presence remains portable.
The testimony is placed within.
The cover of pure gold is made.
Two cherubim are hammered from gold.
They rise from the two ends of the cover.
Their wings shelter the meeting place.
Cover and testimony are joined.
There, between cherubim, YHWH will meet and speak.
A table is made for presence-bread.
Gold surrounds the table.
A rim holds the sacred setting.
Rings prepare the table for movement.
The poles fit near the rim.
The table too is carried through wilderness.
Golden vessels serve the table.
Bread of presence stays before the Face.
A hammered gold lampstand grows like a tree of light.
Six branches extend from the stem.
Almond blossoms open along the branches.
The central lampstand blooms.
Bulbs gather beneath the paired branches.
All is one hammered work of gold.
Seven lamps give light before its face.
The tools for tending light are gold.
The whole lampstand is made from a talent of pure gold.
The pattern shown on the mountain guides the work.

contemplative_note: Exodus 25 begins sacred architecture as willing-hearted presence: ark, table, lampstand, speech between cherubim, bread before the Face, and a golden tree of light.
